units.unit_theme: What's he like? / Phonics: -y ending / Read and write about a character / Unit 1 Check & Wrap-up

core_vocabulary.chinese: （用于女子的姓氏或姓名前，不指明婚否）女士
会说；会讲（某种语言）；用（某种语言）说话

core_patterns: Introduce and describe people using adjectives.
This is Amy. She's quiet. She's very hard-working.
That is Wu Binbin. He's very clever. He's polite, too.
Ms Wang will be our new Chinese teacher.
We have a new PE teacher. He's a good football player.
Listen for key information to identify a person.
Ask and answer about people's identity and characteristics.
Is he young? No, he isn't. He's old.
Is he funny? Yes, he is.
No, I don't. Who is he?
Is she/he ...? Yes, she/he is. / No, she/he isn't.
Is he young? Yes, he is.
She's the head teacher. She's tall. She's strict.
The letter 'y' at the end of a word can sound like /i/.
Choose one sentence from above and write.
Ms Wang is a new teacher here. Listen and tick.

units.unit_theme: My week / Weekend activities / Phonics: ee / ea / Reading, comprehension and writing
core_patterns: What do you have on Thursdays?
I have maths, English and music.
Do you often read books in this park?
What do you have on Fridays?
No, I don't. I don't like sports.
Ask and answer about weekend activities.
Do you often read books on the weekend?
Listen and fill in the blank.
What do you do on Thursdays?
Great! What do you have on Thursdays?
Oh, I love music! Who's your music teacher?
No. He's funny. What do you do on Thursdays, Grandpa?
Practice asking and answering about weekly schedules.
What do we have on Wednesdays?
What do you have on Mondays?
I have Chinese, English, maths and music.
Practice guessing the day of the week based on the schedule.
I have PE, music, science and English.
The letter combinations 'ee' and 'ea' often make the long /i:/ sound.
Choose one sentence from above and write.
It is Saturday morning. Zhang Peng calls Oliver. Listen and tick.
No, I don't. Do you often play football here?
Yes, I do. I like this park very much.
Yes, I do. / No, I don't.
Do you often play ping-pong on the weekend?

core_patterns: What would you like to eat?
What would you like to drink?
I do not like ... but ... is OK.
I do not like ... but I like ...

units.unit_theme: What can you do? / Talents and abilities for a party / Talents and abilities / Phonics: oo

core_vocabulary.chinese: 下一个的；紧接着的；接下来的

core_patterns: What can you do for the party, children?
Can you do any kung fu, John?
What can you do for the party?
We'll have an English party next Tuesday.
Giving a reason for declining an invitation.
What can Mike do? Listen and tick.
We'll have an English party next Tuesday! What can you do for the party, children?
I can do some kung fu!
What can you do, Wu Binbin?
Conduct a class survey about talents.
I can draw cartoons. I can do some kung fu, too!
The letter combination 'oo' can make the long /u:/ sound, as in 'zoo'.
The letter combination 'oo' can also make the short /ʊ/ sound, as in 'book'.
Choose one sentence from above and write.
Can Mike do any kung fu? Listen and tick.
Can you do any kung fu?
Yes, I can. / No, I can't.
Can you do any kung fu, Oliver?
Good afternoon, children. Today we'll learn some kung fu.

units.unit_theme: Describing a room or place / There is a big bed / Describing a bedroom / Describing a room

core_vocabulary.chinese: 祖父；祖母；外祖父；外祖母

core_patterns: There are so many pictures here.
There are many pretty flowers in it.
My father can draw very well.
That's my grandma's garden. There are many pretty flowers in it.
There is a desk beside the bed.
What is in Zhang Peng's bedroom? Listen and tick.
My computer is here on the desk.
There is a nice photo, too.
Using 'There is...' to describe objects in a picture.
There is a desk in the picture.
There is a plate on the desk.
There is a banana on the plate.
This is my room. There is a clock, a ...
Practice using 'There is a/an...' and 'and' to list multiple items.
There is a clock in my room.
There is a clock and a plant in my room.
There is a clock, a plant and a photo in my room.
The letter combinations 'ai' and 'ay' often make the /eɪ/ sound.
Choose one sentence from above and write.
What is in the living room? Listen and tick.
There are so many plants here, too.
Wow! There are so many pictures here.
Yes. My father can draw very well.
They're my grandmother's plants. My grandparents have a garden in front of their house. There are lots of flowers in it.
Practice describing a scene using 'There are...'
There are two dogs in the picture.
It's in front of the dog.

units.unit_theme: In a nature park / Describing a nature park / Describing a park / Phonics: ou / ow
core_patterns: Is there a river in the forest, Miss White?
Are there any tall buildings in the nature park?
There is a big white bird in the sky!
There are many pretty fish in the river.
Look! There is a big white bird in the sky!
Is there a river in the park?
Yes, there is. / No, there isn't.
I'm hungry. Is there a restaurant beside the lake?
No, there isn't. Look! I have some food. Let's share.
What is in the park? Listen and tick the word you hear.
Is there a river in the forest?
Is there a lake, Miss White?
Children, let's go to the forest!
Practice asking and answering 'Is there...?' questions.
Is there a lake in the park?
Practice asking and answering 'Is there...?' questions based on a self-drawn picture.
Is there a river in front of the mountain?
The letter combinations 'ou' and 'ow' can make the /aʊ/ sound.
Choose one sentence from above and write.
Where are the tables? Listen and fill in the blank.
The nature park is so quiet!
Practice asking and answering 'Are there any...?' and 'How many?'
Are there any lakes in the park?
Are there any lakes on the mountain?
Practice writing descriptive sentences using 'There is' and 'There are'.

units.unit_theme: Recycle 1 - Chen Jie at Willow Primary School / Recycle 1 - School Schedule / Recycle 1 - Ordering Lunch / Recycle 1 - Reading and Writing Practice
core_patterns: Review how to ask about and describe people, especially teachers.
What's she like? Is she kind?
Look at that picture. That's Ms Zhang, our Chinese teacher.
Yes. She's very kind, but she's strict sometimes.
Chen Jie meets some new friends at Willow Primary School. Listen to the dialogues and fill in the table.
Listen to the dialogues again. Circle the right answers.
Look at the schedule above. Tick or cross.
Hi. Welcome! What would you like to eat?
What would you like to drink?
A sandwich, please. It's my favourite food. Thanks.
Oh, we don't have tea today. We have tea on Wednesdays and we have milk today.
What would you like to eat?
Monday It is sunny today. I am at Willow Primary School. It is so pretty. There are many yellow flowers.
David is my new friend. He is clever and hard-working. He is funny, too. His favourite class is English. The teacher, Ms Brown, is very helpful.
Teachers and students often eat lunch at school. Some of them have beef and drink tea. Some love green beans and ice cream. The food is delicious!
After lunch, we have a reading class with Mr Reed. What a friendly school!
Find "friends" for the words in the diary.
Rearrange the words. Write down the sentences.

units.unit_theme: Recycle 2 - Having fun in a park / Recycle 2 - Describing a map / Recycle 2 - Story Reading, Retelling and Writing
core_patterns: The children will go to a nature park this weekend. Robin wants to go, too. He calls Miss White. Listen and tick or cross.
Listen again and answer the questions.
What else can Robin do? Write two sentences.
Practice asking 'Can you...?' and answering with 'Yes, I can.' or 'No, I can't.'
Practice reading comprehension and spatial reasoning by drawing based on descriptions.
Are there any lakes in the forest?
Is there a restaurant in the forest?
Are there any villages in the nature park?
Is there a robot in the park?
Practice reading with rhythm and intonation, building suspense.
In the dark, dark wood there is a dark, dark ...
Find 'friends' for the words in the story and write them down.
Write your own story. Read it to your partner.

units.unit_theme: My day / Weekend Activities / Daily schedules / Daily Activities
core_patterns: When do you finish class in the morning?
I often watch TV and play ping-pong with my father.
What do you do on the weekend?
When do you start class in Spain?
I often get up at 7 o'clock.
I always start class at 8 o'clock.
Today is Sunday! What do you do on the weekend?
I often clean my room and wash my clothes. Sometimes I play ping-pong with Zhang Peng.
I often go shopping with my mum.
Where are Zhang Peng and Pedro? Listen and tick.
When do you usually eat dinner in Spain?
Wow! When do you go back to school after lunch?
We finish class at 1 o'clock. Then we eat lunch at home.
Practice asking and answering about daily routines for different professions.
When do you go to bed?
At 6 o'clock in the morning. I work at night.
When do you do morning exercises?
Practice asking and answering questions about daily routines.
Learn the consonant blend 'cl' as in 'clean'.
Learn the consonant blend 'pl' as in 'plate'.
Class, clock, plate, eggplant, clean, play.
Where is Sarah? Listen and circle.
Good girl! So what do you do on the weekend?
My mum worked last night. So I'm shopping today.
That sounds like a lot of fun.
I often clean my room on Saturdays!

units.unit_theme: My favourite season / Favourite Seasons / The Four Seasons / Phonics: br / gr / r
core_patterns: Which season do you like best, Miss White?
Which season do you like best?
Winter. I can sleep all day. How about you?
I like spring best. There are beautiful flowers everywhere.
I like summer best because of Children's Day.
What do you often do in summer?
Look at my picture, Miss White. I like summer best because of Children's Day.
What is the weather like today? Listen and write.
Do you like the music, children?
Yes. It's very beautiful. What is it?
The Four Seasons. Today we'll draw the seasons. Which season do you like best, Mike?
I like snow, too. Which season do you like best, Wu Binbin?
Survey classmates about their favourite season.
Summer. I can go swimming every day!
Learn the consonant blend 'br' as in 'brown'.
Learn the consonant blend 'gr' as in 'green'.
Learn the sound of 'r' in words like 'library' and 'umbrella'.
Zhang Peng and John are looking at some pictures. Which is Zhang Peng's picture? Listen and tick.
Yes. I like autumn best. The weather is good and the colours are beautiful! Which season do you like best, Miss White?
Hello, Miss White. Look at my picture.
Good job! I like the trees. The colours are very pretty!
I like spring best. There are beautiful flowers everywhere. I often go on a picnic with my family.
Winter. I often play in the snow.
Look at the green trees and pink flowers.
I like spring because there are beautiful flowers everywhere.
The weather is hot, hot, hot!

units.unit_theme: My school calendar / Months and Festivals / School Events Calendar / Phonics: ch / sh
core_patterns: It's in October. We'll go to the Great Wall.
When is the trip this year?
It's usually in June. My mum will make zongzi then. I hope you can come and share some with us.
When is the school trip this year?
Is the singing contest in May, too?
Who will go to the sports meet next week? Listen and tick.
We have a few fun things in spring. After the sports meet, we have an English party.
It's in April. We have a school trip, too.
Learn the consonant digraph 'ch' /tʃ/ as in 'China'.
Learn the consonant digraph 'sh' /ʃ/ as in 'sheep'.
What will Oliver have? Listen and tick.
We'll go to the Great Wall.
Great! When is the trip this year?
I like autumn. I really like the colours.
I like autumn, too. We usually have a school trip in autumn.
I'll cook for my mother. What about you?
It's usually in September or October. I'll eat mooncakes with my family.

English Party WHEN: April 12th, 7 p.m. WHERE: Gym WHAT: You will play games. You will read riddles. You will act out stories. You will meet Robin the Robot. He is a great English teacher! RSVP: Will you come to the party? Please send an email by March 23rd.

When is Mid-Autumn Festival this year?
What do you usually do on Mid-Autumn Festival?
We eat mooncakes. My family will also eat a birthday cake.
My cousin's birthday is on Mid-Autumn Festival this year!
Which month are they talking about? Listen and tick.
It's Labour Day. When is the reading festival?
There are some other special days in May.
The school art show and the reading festival.
Learn the voiced 'th' sound /ð/ as in 'this'.
Learn the unvoiced 'th' sound /θ/ as in 'three'.
When is the baby's birthday? Listen and tick.
What will you do for your mum?
Cool! What will you do for your mum?
Learn to write ordinal number abbreviations (st, nd, rd, th).

units.unit_theme: Whose dog is it? / Possession / Possessive Adjectives and Pronouns / Phonics: -ng / -nk
core_patterns: Wow! What a beautiful painting! Is it yours?
Oh, it's mine. He's sleeping. He's very tired.
I see. Why is it jumping?
Listen and tick. Which is John's picture?
The yellow picture is mine. Are these all ours?
Wow! That picture of Beijing is beautiful. Whose is it?
Learn the difference between possessive adjectives (my, your, his, her, its, our, their) and possessive pronouns (mine, yours, his, hers, its, ours, theirs).
Yes! It's your dog. The dog is yours.
Listen and tick. Where is the dog?
Can I play with him now?
Yes. Can you take him to the park?

units.unit_theme: Work quietly! / Describing animals' actions / Daily Activities and Time / Phonics: wh

core_vocabulary.chinese: （指事物、动物或幼儿）它的；他的；她的

core_patterns: Following rules in a classroom or library.
Using imperative sentences to give commands.
Haha. Look! They're eating lunch! They're so cute.
Asking and answering about someone's location and current activity.
She's listening to music in the classroom.
Haha. They're eating lunch! They're so cute.
It's 7:30 p.m. What's Chen Jie doing?
She's listening to music. It's 9 o'clock. What are the students doing?
Learn the pronunciation of the 'wh' digraph in question words.
Using imperative sentences to state rules.
Can I read the books here?
My name is Tom. What's your name?
OK. Can I read the books here?
Shh. Talk quietly. I'm John. I can show you the English books.

core_patterns: It is Saturday morning. Sarah is calling her friends. Listen and write the names.
Practice making plans over the phone.
Hi, Oliver. This is Wu Binbin. Are you free this ...?
When do you go to bed, then?
Wow! When do you eat lunch?
When do you go to work?
Yes, I'm a doctor. I often work at night.
When do you get up, Mum?
I usually get up at 6:00 a.m.

Hi, Sarah! It is autumn in April here in Sydney. Autumn is my favourite season! It is warm in the day and cool at night. Many people visit us in autumn because they like the weather. When you come, you can visit the Sydney Opera House. It is the most famous building in Australia. What is the weather like in April in your city? What do you often do? All the best, Stella
Now can you answer the email for Sarah, please?
Bed in Summer by Robert Louis Stevenson
In winter I get up at night And dress by yellow candlelight.
In summer, quite the other way, I have to go to bed by day.
I have to go to bed and see The birds still hopping on the tree,
Or hear the grown-up people's feet Still going past me in the street.
And does it not seem hard to you, When all the sky is clear and blue,

I see. How can I get there?
Excuse me. Can you help me?
How can I get to the science museum?
I don't know. I'll ask. Excuse me, sir.
Wow! A talking robot! What a great museum!
There is a pet hospital in my city.
Wu Binbin is calling Mike. Listen and tick or cross.
Yum! I like pizza! Where is the restaurant?
Yes, but I'm hungry now. I know a great Italian restaurant.
How can I get to the ... ?
Where is the Italian restaurant? Turn right here?
Go straight and you can see ...

units.unit_theme: Ways to go to school
core_patterns: How do you come to school?
Don't go at the red light!
I must pay attention to the traffic lights!
In the USA people on bikes must wear one.
How do you go to school?
Good morning, John. How do you go to school?
Wait! Don't go at the red light!
Hi, Mike. Is this your bike?
Thanks. How do you come to school?
Hi, children. You're early. How do you come to school?
How do you come to school, Mrs Smith? By car?
Let's go to the nature park.
How do you get to the USA from China?
What does Mrs Smith tell the children to do? Listen and tick.
How can I get to the Fuxing Hospital?
Take the No. 57 bus over there.
Mr Jones, how can I get to the Fuxing Hospital?
You can see this on the road/in the subway.
You must stop. You can see this on the road.
Don't touch the door. You can see this in the subway.
Slow down and stop at a yellow light.
Stop and wait at a red light.
Look! The park is over there! Let's go!

units.unit_theme: My weekend plan
core_patterns: What are you going to do tomorrow?
I'm going to have an art lesson.
We're going to draw some pictures in Renmin Park.
What are you going to do in the nature park?
I'm going to draw some pictures.
I'm going to look for some beautiful leaves.
I'm going to draw some pictures. What about you?
What am I going to do this weekend?
Mike is going to see a film.
John is going to buy his favourite comic book.
Mike is going to see a film. John is going to buy his favourite comic book. Hmm, what am I going to do this weekend?
I'm going to see a film tomorrow.
What are you going to do in your lesson?
Sounds great! I'm going to see a film tomorrow.
What are you going to do today?
I'm going to see a film.
What are you going to do this morning?
I'm going to visit my grandparents.
My cousin Jack is going to visit me next week.
We're going to see a film about space travel!
That's nice. Where are you going?
Cool! I have lots of comic books about space. When are you going?
Where are you going this afternoon?
What are you going to do there?
I'm going to buy a new comic book.
To the bookstore. I'm going to buy a new comic book.

Yes, he does. He lives on a farm. Look!

He likes reading stories. He lives on a farm, so sometimes he reads to the cows!
My best friend is Xu Wei.
He likes flying kites and singing English songs.

I also like singing and doing kung fu.

We should study hard and stay healthy.

Here you are. I'll go and get some drinks. Wait for me.

Mum, I feel ill. Should I count to ten, too?
Sarah is angry. The cat is afraid.

units.unit_theme: How tall are you? / Comparatives / How can I get there?

core_vocabulary.chinese: （short 的比较级）更矮的；更短的

core_patterns: That's the tallest dinosaur in this hall.
It's taller than both of us together.
What size are your shoes, Mike?
Your feet are bigger than mine. My shoes are size 37.
Some dinosaurs are bigger than houses.
I'm 21 metres tall. I'm the tallest!
I'm 1.6 metres tall. I'm taller than this dinosaur.
The children are in the museum. Listen and circle.
Yes, it is. How tall is it?
Oh, yes. How tall are you?
Wu Binbin is taller than John, but Zhang Peng is taller than Wu Binbin.
Your feet are bigger than mine.
But I think you are heavier. How heavy are you?

core_patterns: I stayed at home with your grandma. We drank tea in the afternoon and watched TV.
Yes, I cleaned my room and washed my clothes.
I want to buy the new film magazine.
No, I had a cold. I stayed at home all weekend and slept.
What did you do last weekend? Did you see a film?
What did you do last weekend?
Did you play football with Zhang Peng?
I cleaned my room and washed my clothes on Saturday.
Yes, I did. We played football on Sunday.
Hi, Grandpa. How are you? How was your weekend?
That's nice. Did you do anything else?
I'm fine, Mike. It was good, thank you.
What did Mike's grandpa do last weekend?
What are you going to do next weekend?
I stayed at home and watched TV.
I stayed at home all weekend and slept.
Yes, it talked about a lot of new films. What did you do last weekend? Did you see a film?
Hey, Amy. Let's go to the bookstore. I want to buy the new film magazine.
What did you do yesterday/last night ...?
What do you usually do on weekends?
Yes, I did. It was great.

You didn't come to school. What happened?
Oh dear! Are you all right?
Can I come and visit you?
Yes, I did. I saw lots of grapes and ate lots of mutton kebabs. They were delicious.
John answered a phone call from Amy. Listen and tick or cross.
That's too bad! Are you all right?
Oh, yes. It looks like a mule! Did you go to Turpan?
Where did John go over his holiday? What did he do?
Where did you go over your holiday?
Where did you go last Saturday?
I went to a forest park.
Where did you go over the winter holiday?
Hainan is far from here. How did you go there?
How was the beach? What did you do there?
Where did Amy go last winter holiday?
Amy: How was your summer holiday, Binbin?
Wu Binbin: It was good. I went swimming every weekend and took lots of pictures.

units.unit_theme: Then and now / What a dream! / Changes are all around us / Story time
core_patterns: There was no library in my old school.
Tell us about your school, please.
There were no computers or Internet in my time.
Before, I was quiet. Now, I'm very active in class.
I was short, so I couldn't ride my bike well. Now, I go cycling every day.
I didn't like PE class very much.
I couldn't run fast or play basketball well.
You weren't very big then, Mr Wu!
No, so I didn't like PE class very much. I couldn't run fast or play basketball well.
We didn't have a library, and there weren't any computer rooms at all.
Could you see stars at night?
Wow, a library! There was no library in my old school.
What was your school like five years ago?
There was no gym in my school thirty years ago.
Now there's a new one in our school.
At that time, there were no gyms.
I was short, so I couldn't ride my bike well.
Right. Before, I was quiet. Now, I'm very active in class. How about you?
John, come and look at Mike's old photos!
That's funny, Mike! You're wearing a pink T-shirt.

Identify and name items in the picture.

Bill is my new friend. He's short and heavy.
Use the simple present tense to describe daily routines and activities.
Practice collocations with go, do, play, and have.
Describe ongoing actions using the present continuous tense.
Use past tense verbs to describe daily activities.
Use time sequence words: then, after that, next, finally.
Compare sizes using comparatives and superlatives.
Guess! Is this bigger or smaller than the elephant?
I think it's bigger than the elephant.
No, it isn't. It's smaller. Look. It's a tiger.
Look. The other card is a whale.
Talk about school life, subjects, and favorite classes.
Identify factual errors based on a reading passage.
